edgeCases: osFS treats every os error as absent: Stat returns ok=false on a missing path (no ModTime leaked), ReadFile returns ok=false on missing/unreadable, Exists is os.Stat==nil — so the production adapter matches the FS contract Decide relies on for missing files.
DecisionEmit reached at runtime when MarkerPath is absent: NewOSFS().ReadFile returns ok=false, Decide short-circuits to Emit before any mtime comparison.

DecisionStale reached two ways through the real FS: (a) marker JSON unparseable or 'at' not RFC3339 -> parseMarkerAt ok=false -> Stale; (b) marker parses but LatestMtime(RequiredArtifacts) is After(markerAt) -> Stale. Both print the identical directive+panel; only the internal Decision differs.

DecisionSuppressed reached when marker 'at' >= latest artifact mtime, OR FirstIncompleteBootstrap returns ('',false) (no/empty Phase 0 or all done), OR .workflow/<first>.json already exists (user picked start). emitRoadmapCheckpoint returns silently in every suppress case.

Anti-spam: writing the marker via 'centinela roadmap iterate' sets at=now().UTC(); next hook run sees latest mtime <= at -> Suppressed, so the directive does not re-fire until a roadmap artifact mtime moves past the marker.

Marker idempotency: WriteMarker uses os.WriteFile (truncate) with fixed {choice:iterate,at:<rfc3339>}; re-running 'centinela roadmap iterate' overwrites in place, never appends, last writer wins with equivalent content.
Hook never blocks: emitRoadmapCheckpoint has no error return and is called as the last statement before 'return nil'; a Suppressed decision produces zero output and the command still exits zero.

Roadmap loaded once: hook_setup reuses the r,err := roadmap.Load() value from the JSON-validity branch, so the checkpoint branch only runs after roadmap.json parsed successfully (invalid json hits roadmapJSONDirective and returns first, preserving directive precedence).

UI/decision separation: render_roadmap_checkpoint.go imports only lipgloss and takes featureName as a string param; it never imports roadmapcheckpoint, keeping all emit/suppress logic out of internal/ui (G7).
